+
+## Context length
+
+`OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH=65536` was set on 2026-07-18 because the hermes-agent
+(`10-hermes`) needs at least 64k. Without it Ollama auto-sized the window to
+32768, while hermes auto-detected 262144 from the model metadata and packed
+prompts to fit — every response came back with
+`finish_reason='length'` (`⚠️ Response truncated`).
+
+VRAM budget on `qwen3.6:35b` (36B MoE, Q4_K_M): weights occupy 21692 MiB of
+the 32768 MiB card with all 42 layers on GPU, leaving ~11 GB for KV cache and
+compute buffers. Raising context further, or loading a denser model alongside,
+risks CPU offload — check `ollama ps` (the `CONTEXT` column) and `nvidia-smi`
+after any change.
+
+Unresolved: whether `OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=2` allocates 65536 per slot or splits
+it across both. If it splits, clients see 32k and the truncation returns.

1	# Fleet GitOps Handoff — Service Configs for k3s Cluster
2	
3	**Purpose:** This document gives you (the local LLM) the information needed to help
4	write Fleet-ready GitOps configs for the listed services and commit them to the
5	`homelab-fleet` GitHub repo via the GitHub MCP tools.
6	
7	**Cluster basics:**
8	- Single-node k3s on openSUSE Leap 16 host (Podman, not Docker).
9	- Host LAN IP: `192.168.7.149`. MetalLB pool on the `192.168.7.x` range.
10	- Storage class: `longhorn`. Most app namespace: `ai`.
11	- GPUs: Tesla V100 (`GPU-a69c6398-0353-3234-9f35-af0e02865f33`),
12	  GTX 1070 (`GPU-5641c03a-6749-62ab-cd11-fd3cd34d29f4`).
13	
14	---
15	
16	## ⚠️ CRITICAL — Two categories of service
17	
18	The services below fall into two groups. **Treat them differently.**
19	
20	### Group A — configs are KNOWN (provided in this doc)
21	These were built recently and their manifests are documented here accurately:
22	`node-red`, `resilio`, `emby`, `k8s-mcp-server`.
23	
24	For these: the config details in this doc are reliable. You can use them to write
25	Fleet manifests. Still verify against the live cluster with the k8s MCP tools
26	before committing (image tags or tweaks may have changed).
27	
28	### Group B — configs are NOT known (you MUST read them from the cluster)
29	These were deployed BEFORE this info was captured. **The details are NOT provided
30	because they are not known.** Do NOT invent or guess values for these:
31	`ollama`, `milvus`, `attu`, `open-webui`.
32	
33	For these you MUST pull the real running config from the cluster using the
34	Kubernetes MCP tools (read-only). Steps:
35	1. Use the k8s MCP tools to `listResources` / `getResource` for Deployments,
36	   StatefulSets, Services, PVCs, ConfigMaps in the `ai` namespace matching each app.
37	2. For Helm-deployed services (likely `ollama` and `milvus`), the rendered
38	   manifests are what the cluster shows — but the GitOps source-of-truth is the
39	   Helm values. Flag to the user that Helm values must be captured with
40	   `helm -n ai get values <release>` (you may not be able to run helm via the
41	   read-only k8s MCP — tell the user to run it and paste the output).
42	3. STRIP runtime fields before writing to Git: `status`, `metadata.uid`,
43	   `metadata.resourceVersion`, `metadata.creationTimestamp`,
44	   `metadata.managedFields`, `spec.clusterIP` (let it re-allocate), and any
45	   `ownerReferences`. These do not belong in GitOps source.
46	
47	**Do not fabricate Group B configs. If you cannot read them, say so and ask the
48	user to provide them. A wrong GitOps config will be applied to the cluster by
49	Fleet.**
50	
51	---
52	
53	## Group A — Known configs
54	
55	### node-red
56	- Namespace: `node-red` (dedicated). Image: `nodered/node-red:latest`.
57	- Storage: Longhorn PVC `node-red-data` (RWO, 5Gi) mounted at `/data`.
58	- Runs as uid/gid 1000; pod securityContext `runAsUser/Group/fsGroup: 1000`
59	  (the official image takes runAsUser cleanly).
60	- Env: `TZ=America/Chicago`.
61	- Port: 1880. Service: LoadBalancer (MetalLB).
62	- Strategy: Recreate (single RWO writer).
63	- Note: editor is unauthenticated by default — user should set adminAuth in
64	  /data/settings.js.
65	
66	### resilio (Resilio Sync)
67	- Namespace: `resilio`. Image: `resilio/sync:latest`.
68	- Storage: hostPath `/data/resilio` (restored prior deployment — config + identity).
69	- Key in-container paths (from the restored sync.conf):
70	  - storage_path = `/mnt/sync/config`  (host `/data/resilio/config`)
71	  - folders whitelist = `/mnt/sync/folders` (host `/data/resilio/folders`)
72	  - config file = `/mnt/sync/sync.conf` (host `/data/resilio/sync.conf`)
73	  - webui listen = `0.0.0.0:8888`; listening_port = 55555
74	- On-disk ownership uid 471 / gid 100 -> PUID/PGID and runAsUser must match.
75	- Command: `rslsync --nodaemon --config /mnt/sync/sync.conf`.
76	- Ports: 8888 (webui), 55555 (sync). Service: LoadBalancer (MetalLB).
77	- Strategy: Recreate (single hostPath writer).
78	- Mount the whole tree subdirs to match sync.conf's expected in-container paths.
79	
80	### emby (Emby Media Server)
81	- Namespace: `emby`. Image: `emby/embyserver:latest`.
82	- This is an s6-overlay image: it MUST start as root and drop to UID/GID itself.
83	  DO NOT set a pod-level runAsUser/securityContext — it breaks s6 setup. Use env
84	  `UID`/`GID` (and `PUID`/`PGID`) = 472 / 473 instead.
85	- Storage (hostPath, restored prior deployment):
86	  - host `/data/emby`   -> container `/config`  (config/db, owner 472:473)
87	  - host `/data/stream` -> container `/data/stream` (media; DB expects this exact
88	    path — 1:1 mount keeps libraries intact)
89	- GPU transcode: GTX 1070 pinned via `NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=GPU-5641c03a-...`
90	  with `NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=compute,utility,video` (the `video` cap is
91	  required for NVENC/NVDEC). `runtimeClassName: nvidia`, `nvidia.com/gpu: 1`.
92	- Ports: 8096 (http), 8920 (https). Service: LoadBalancer (MetalLB).
93	- Strategy: Recreate.
94	
95	### k8s-mcp-server
96	- Namespace: `ai`. Image: `ginnux/k8s-mcp-server:latest`.
97	- Deployed READ-ONLY: `--mode streamable-http --port 8080 --read-only`.
98	- ServiceAccount `k8s-mcp-server-sa` + a read-only ClusterRole (get/list/watch
99	  only; no create/update/patch/delete) + ClusterRoleBinding.
100	- Service: ClusterIP on 8080 (in-cluster; reached by mcpo).
101	- Bridged to Open-WebUI via mcpo (OpenAPI), not connected directly.
102	
103	---
104	
105	## Group B — Read these from the cluster (NOT provided; do not guess)
106	
107	For each, use the k8s MCP tools to read the live objects in namespace `ai`, then
108	strip runtime fields (listed above) before writing Fleet manifests.
109	
110	### ollama
111	- Deployed via Helm (chart `ollama-helm/ollama`). Pinned to the V100 by UUID.
112	- KNOWN hints (verify against cluster, do not treat as complete):
113	  - Engine pinned to V100 via `NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=GPU-a69c6398-...`
114	  - Service type LoadBalancer; PVC ~100Gi Longhorn; serves :11434.
115	  - Models are stored on the PVC, not in config (don't need to encode them).
116	- ACTION: get Helm values (`helm -n ai get values ollama`) for the GitOps source,
117	  AND read the rendered Deployment/Service/PVC for reference. Prefer a Fleet
118	  HelmRelease (chart + values) over raw manifests for this one.
119	
120	### milvus
121	- Deployed via Helm (standalone, v2.6.x). Backed by Longhorn PVCs (data, etcd,
122	  minio). Service on :19530.
123	- ACTION: get Helm values (`helm -n ai get values milvus`) and read the rendered
124	  resources. Prefer a Fleet HelmRelease. Do NOT hand-write Milvus manifests —
125	  it's a multi-component chart.
126	
127	### attu
128	- Milvus admin UI. Image `zilliz/attu` (version is independent of Milvus version).
129	- ACTION: read the live Deployment + Service from the cluster. Likely a simple
130	  Deployment + Service; capture exact image tag and the MILVUS_URL env it uses.
131	
132	### open-webui
133	- Deployed as a StatefulSet (pod `open-webui-0`), NOT a Deployment. Has a Longhorn
134	  PVC. Serves the chat UI. Has `WEBUI_SECRET_KEY` set (required so stored tool
135	  credentials survive restarts).
136	- ACTION: read the live StatefulSet + Service + PVC. Capture the exact image tag,
137	  env (especially WEBUI_SECRET_KEY handling — keep the secret in a k8s Secret,
138	  NOT inline in Git), and the PVC name. Flag any secrets to the user to store
139	  via a Secret, not committed plaintext.
140	
141	---
142	
143	## Fleet repo structure (suggested)
144	
145	Target repo: `homelab-fleet`. A common Fleet layout:
146	
147	```
148	homelab-fleet/
149	  fleet.yaml                 # top-level bundle config (optional defaults)
150	  node-red/
151	    fleet.yaml
152	    node-red.yaml
153	  resilio/
154	    fleet.yaml
155	    resilio-sync.yaml
156	  emby/
157	    fleet.yaml
158	    emby-server.yaml
159	  k8s-mcp-server/
160	    fleet.yaml
161	    k8s-mcp-server.yaml
162	  ollama/
163	    fleet.yaml               # HelmRelease-style: chart ref + values
164	  milvus/
165	    fleet.yaml               # HelmRelease-style
166	  attu/
167	    fleet.yaml
168	    attu.yaml
169	  open-webui/
170	    fleet.yaml
171	    open-webui.yaml
172	```
173	
174	Each app dir gets a `fleet.yaml` telling Fleet how to deploy that path. For Helm
175	apps (ollama, milvus), the `fleet.yaml` references the chart + values rather than
176	raw manifests.
177	
178	---
179	
180	## Hard rules for writing these configs
181	
182	1. **Never invent Group B values.** Read them from the cluster. If you can't, ask.
183	2. **Strip runtime fields** (status, uid, resourceVersion, creationTimestamp,
184	   managedFields, clusterIP, ownerReferences) from anything read via kubectl/MCP.
185	3. **Secrets do NOT go in Git as plaintext.** Any token/password/secret-key
186	   (e.g. open-webui's WEBUI_SECRET_KEY, the github-mcp-token) must be a k8s Secret
187	   referenced by name — flag these to the user to create/seal separately
188	   (e.g. SealedSecrets or sops) rather than committing values.
189	4. **Helm services use HelmRelease/values**, not rendered manifests, as GitOps
190	   source.
191	5. **Spot-check before commit.** Fleet reconciles Git -> cluster, so a wrong file
192	   gets applied. Summarize what each file will do before writing it.
193	6. Use `create_or_update_file` or `push_files` to write to `homelab-fleet`. You do
194	   NOT have repo-create, fork, or file-delete tools (deliberately excluded).
195

1 # lab-fleet — Home Lab Cluster Configuration as Code 2 3 The complete, install-ordered configuration of a single-node k3s cluster running a private AI platform: local LLM inference on a Tesla V100, RAG over Milvus, agentic MCP tooling, GPU image generation, and the home services that share the node. Every file was curated from **live cluster state** — helm values pulled from running releases, manifests reconstructed from applied objects, and application sources recovered from container image layers after their Dockerfiles were lost. 4 5 This repo is mounted as the [`lab/` submodule of ash4d.com](https://github.com/darthzen/ash4d.com), which carries the full architecture documentation. This repo carries the configs. 6 7 ## Installation Order 8 9 ```mermaid 10 flowchart TD 11 subgraph host["00 — Host (openSUSE Leap 16.0)"] 12 DRIVER[NVIDIA G06 driver + CDI] 13 K3S[k3s v1.35] 14 end 15 16 subgraph platform["01-03 — Platform"] 17 METALLB[01 MetalLB
+ Traefik] 18 LONGHORN[02 Longhorn] 19 GPU[03 RuntimeClass
+ Device Plugin + DCGM] 20 end 21 22 subgraph ai["04-09 — AI Stack (namespace: ai)"] 23 OLLAMA[04 Ollama

I read the handoff (`~/Downloads/fleet-handoff.md`), and before executing anything I need to flag something important: **the handoff's work already appears to be done — and superseded by the repo you're sitting in.**

## What the handoff asks for

It's a doc written for a *local LLM* to author "Fleet-ready GitOps configs" for 8 services — `node-red`, `resilio`, `emby`, `k8s-mcp-server`, `ollama`, `milvus`, `attu`, `open-webui` — and **push them to a separate `homelab-fleet` GitHub repo via GitHub MCP tools**.

## What actually exists

Every one of those 8 services already has config-as-code in **this** `lab-fleet` repo:

| Handoff target | Already in repo |
|---|---|
| node-red | `12-node-red/node-red.yaml` |
| resilio | `13-resilio/resilio.yaml` |
| emby | `11-emby/emby.yaml` |
| k8s-mcp-server | `09-mcp/k8s-mcp-server.yaml` |
| ollama (Helm) | `04-ollama/values.yaml` |
| milvus (Helm) | `06-milvus/values.yaml` |
| attu | `06-milvus/attu.yaml` |
| open-webui | `05-open-webui/values.yaml` |

The initial commit — *"install-ordered lab config-as-code (00-14), curated from live cluster state"* — is essentially the executed (and improved) result of this handoff.

## Why I stopped instead of executing

Taking the handoff literally would mean creating a **redundant, separate `homelab-fleet` repo** and re-deriving configs that already exist here (more current — e.g. the V100 is now documented as 32 GB, ollama values were bumped today). It also can't be done as written: the git remote here is `.../gh/darthzen/lab-fleet` (not `homelab-fleet`), no GitHub MCP tools are loaded, and pushing a new public repo is an outward-facing action I shouldn't take on a stale doc without checking.

**One arguable gap:** the handoff wanted true Fleet *bundle* structure (a `fleet.yaml` per app). This repo ships plain manifests + Helm `values.yaml` (the `helm upgrade --install` pattern), not Fleet `fleet.yaml` wrappers — even though step 14 references a Rancher/Fleet mgmt plane.

This is the still-open half of the scope doc's A/B question and the stronger portfolio line ("and Fleet deploys from this repo"). 6 7 --- 8 9 ## Discovered state (live, 2026-07-06) 10 11 Pulled read-only from the downstream API (`192.168.7.149:6443`, `system:admin` cert) and the cluster-agent env. 12 13 | Fact | Value | Source | 14 |---|---|---| 15 | Rancher server | `v2.14.2` at `https://rancher.ash4d.com` (**192.168.7.148** — separate host) | cattle-cluster-agent env `CATTLE_SERVER` / `CATTLE_SERVER_VERSION` | 16 | ash4d lab node | `sdf1`, k3s `v1.35.5+k3s1`, single node, `node-role…control-plane=true` | `/version`, `/api/v1/nodes` | 17 | Cluster role | **Downstream** of Rancher (cluster id `c-nnzn9`), cattle-cluster-agent + fleet-agent connected | helm release `fleet-agent-c-nnzn9`, cluster-agent env | 18 | fleet-agent | `rancher/fleet-agent:v0.15.2`, Deployment **Ready 1/1** | `cattle-fleet-system` deploys | 19 | **Active GitOps here** | **None** — no `fleet.cattle.io` CRDs on the downstream, no `cluster-fleet-*` namespaces, no BundleDeployments | `/apis/fleet.cattle.io/v1alpha1` → 404; CRD + namespace lists | 20 | Feature hint | cluster-agent env shows `CATTLE_FEATURES=…fleet=false…` | cattle-cluster-agent env | 21 22 **Read:** the cluster is registered to Rancher and the fleet-agent is healthy, but Continuous Delivery has never been wired — no repo deploys here. Targeting `lab-fleet` directly sidesteps this. `ash4d.com` stays the documentation showcase; `lab-fleet` is the deploy source. (This resolves the scope doc's A/B toward "split, deploy from lab-fleet.") 55 56 2. **Public repo → no clone secret.** `lab-fleet` is public, so the GitRepo needs no SSH/HTTP credential. One less secret on the controller. 57 58 3. **Stage by risk; adopt hand-installed Helm releases last.** Everything in `lab-fleet` was hand-installed. Fleet taking over a live Helm release is the only genuinely risky step (ownership handoff, possible pod churn). Raw-manifest components go first; storage/GPU/critical infra goes last or stays monitor-only. 59 60 4. **`lab-fleet` needs `fleet.yaml` files to be deployable.** Today it holds helm *values* and raw manifests, not Fleet bundles. Each component dir needs a `fleet.yaml` (helm chart+version+valuesFiles, or a raw-manifest bundle). This is the bulk of the repo work and is agent-executable under the existing ticket workflow. 61 62 --- 63 64 ## Phase 0 — Controller access + enable CD (owner: Rick; ~15 min) 65 66 Needs the Rancher control plane (`.148`), which the sandbox can't authenticate to. 67 68 1. **Mint a Rancher API token** — UI: avatar → *Account & API Keys* → *Create API Key* (no scope = all clusters), or: 69 ```bash 70 # login → bearer token 71 curl -sk https://rancher.ash4d.com/v3-public/localProviders/local?action=login \ 72 -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ 73 -d '{"username":"admin","password":""}' | jq -r .token # -> token-xxxxx:secret 74 export RTOKEN='token-xxxxx:secret' 75 ``` 76 2. **Verify the fleet feature is on** (the agent env hinted `fleet=false`): 77 ```bash 78 curl -sk https://rancher.ash4d.com/v3/features/fleet -H "Authorization: Bearer $RTOKEN" | jq '{name:.id,value:.value,default:.default}' 79 # if disabled, enable: 80 curl -sk -X PUT https://rancher.ash4d.com/v3/features/fleet \ 81 -H "Authorization: Bearer $RTOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ 82 -d '{"value":true}' 83 ``` 84 Enabling installs the Fleet controller/CRDs and the CD area in the UI. Give it a minute to reconcile. 85 3. **Confirm the cluster's Fleet identity + labels** (for targeting): 86 ```bash 87 curl -sk https://rancher.ash4d.com/v1/fleet.cattle.io.clusters \ 88 -H "Authorization: Bearer $RTOKEN" | jq '.data[]|{ns:.metadata.namespace,name:.metadata.name,labels:.metadata.labels,cluster:.status.agentDeployedComponents}' 89 # note the workspace namespace (likely fleet-default) and a stable label to target (e.g. management.cattle.io/cluster-name=c-nnzn9) 90 ``` 91 92 **Acceptance:** `GET /v1/fleet.cattle.io.gitrepos` returns 200 (CRD present), and the lab cluster is listed under a Fleet workspace with a targetable label. 93 94 --- 95 96 ## Phase 1 — Make lab-fleet deployable (owner: agent-executable; per-component) 97 98 Add a `fleet.yaml` to each numbered dir. Two shapes: 99 100 **Helm-backed component** (`01-networking`, `02-longhorn`, `03-gpu` nvdp, `04-ollama`, `05-open-webui`, `06-milvus`): 101 ```yaml 102 # 04-ollama/fleet.yaml 103 defaultNamespace: ai 104 helm: 105 releaseName: ollama 106 repo: https://otwld.github.io/ollama-helm/ 107 chart: ollama 108 version: 1.60.0 # pin to the running chart (from helm/releases.json) 109 valuesFiles: 110 - values.yaml 111 takeOwnership: true # adopt the hand-installed release (see risk note) 112 ``` 113 114 **Raw-manifest component** (`03-gpu` runtimeclass, `07-comfyui`, `09-mcp`, `10-hermes`…`13-resilio`, netpol): 115 ```yaml 116 # 07-comfyui/fleet.yaml 117 defaultNamespace: ai 118 # no helm block → Fleet applies the YAML in this path as-is 119 ``` 120 121 Notes: 122 - Secrets stay out of git (already `secretKeyRef`/`.example`); create them out-of-band before the bundle lands, or Fleet will report the workload as not-ready. 123 - Pin every chart `version` to what's live (`lab-capture/.../helm/releases.json`) so Fleet adopts rather than upgrades. 124 - `00-host` and `14-cluster-mgmt` are documentation, not deployables — exclude via the GitRepo `paths` (below), don't add `fleet.yaml`. 125 126 **Acceptance (per dir):** `fleet.yaml` present; `kubeconform`/`helm template` dry-run clean. Fits the existing agent ticket model (qwen3 for the YAML, sonnet for any adoption debugging). 127 128 --- 129 130 ## Phase 2 — Create the GitRepo (owner: Rick or agent w/ token; Rancher API) 131 132 Start **narrow** — a single low-risk path — then widen `paths` as each component is verified. 133 134 ```yaml 135 # gitrepo-lab-fleet.yaml (applied in the Fleet workspace, e.g. fleet-default) 136 apiVersion: fleet.cattle.io/v1alpha1 137 kind: GitRepo 138 metadata: 139 name: lab-fleet 140 namespace: fleet-default 141 spec: 142 repo: https://github.com/darthzen/lab-fleet 143 branch: main 144 paths: 145 - 12-node-red # phase-3 start: low-risk, likely not helm-adopted 146 targets: 147 - clusterSelector: 148 matchLabels: 149 management.cattle.io/cluster-name: c-nnzn9 # confirm exact label in Phase 0 150 ``` 151 152 Create it via the Rancher Steve API (equivalent to UI → *Continuous Delivery → Git Repos → Create*): 153 ```bash 154 curl -sk -X POST https://rancher.ash4d.com/v1/fleet.cattle.io.gitrepos \ 155 -H "Authorization: Bearer $RTOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ 156 --data-binary @<(yq -o=json gitrepo-lab-fleet.yaml) 157 ``` 158 159 **Acceptance:** `GET /v1/fleet.cattle.io.gitrepos/fleet-default/lab-fleet | jq .status` shows `Active`, `readyBundleDeployments` climbing. 160 161 --- 162 163 ## Phase 3 — Staged rollout (owner: Rick approves each widen) 164 165 Widen `spec.paths` one component at a time, riskiest last: 166 167 | Order | Component(s) | Type | Risk | Note | 168 |---|---|---|---|---| 169 | 1 | `12-node-red`, `13-resilio`, `11-emby` | raw/helm apps | low | app-layer, easy rollback | 170 | 2 | `07-comfyui`, `09-mcp`, `10-hermes` | raw manifests | low–med | secrets must pre-exist | 171 | 3 | `05-open-webui`, `06-milvus`, `04-ollama` | helm adopt | med | `takeOwnership`; watch for pod churn | 172 | 4 | `01-networking`, `03-gpu` | helm adopt | med–high | MetalLB/Traefik + GPU; network blips possible | 173 | 5 | `02-longhorn` | helm adopt | **high** | storage — adopt last, or leave monitor-only | 174 175 At each step: add the path, let Fleet render, confirm the BundleDeployment goes Ready, spot-check the workload, then proceed. Roll back by removing the path (Fleet reverts its bundle) or deleting the GitRepo. 176 177 --- 178 179 ## Phase 4 — Verify (either credential works) 180 181 ```bash 182 # controller view 183 curl -sk https://rancher.ash4d.com/v1/fleet.cattle.io.gitrepos/fleet-default/lab-fleet \ 184 -H "Authorization: Bearer $RTOKEN" | jq '.status.summary' 185 # downstream view (using the k3s kubeconfig) 186 kubectl --context lab -n cattle-fleet-system get bundledeployments -A 187 ``` 188 Confirm: bundles Ready, no unexpected restarts on adopted releases, GPU/storage healthy. 189 190 --- 191 192 ## Risks & mitigations 193 194 - **Helm adoption is the real hazard.** Fleet taking ownership of a hand-installed release can trigger a redeploy if the rendered manifest differs from what's live. *Mitigation:* pin chart versions to the running ones, diff `helm get manifest` vs `helm template` before enabling a helm path, and adopt one release at a time. Longhorn last. 195 - **Fleet feature currently looks off** (`fleet=false` hint). *Mitigation:* Phase 0 verifies and enables on the controller. 196 - **Submodule trap.** A GitRepo on `ash4d.com/lab` clones an empty gitlink. *Mitigation:* target `lab-fleet` directly (decision #1). 197 - **Missing secrets = stuck bundles.** Raw manifests reference `secretKeyRef`. *Mitigation:* create the referenced secrets out-of-band before adding those paths. 198 - **Sandbox can't touch the controller.** All controller-side steps are Rick-run (or agent-run) with a Rancher token; I can drive downstream verification with the k3s kubeconfig. 199 200 ## What I can do next (with a Rancher API token) 201 202 Given a Rancher token for `.148`, I can run Phase 0 discovery, author all the `fleet.yaml` files (Phase 1) as a ticket batch on the lab-fleet board, and generate the exact GitRepo payload — leaving only the approve-and-widen calls to you. Without the token, Phases 0/2 are yours; Phase 1 I can do now against the repo. 203 204 ## Portfolio payoff 205 206 Completing this converts the lab from "config-as-code, published" to "**GitOps-reconciled from git by Fleet on a live multi-workload cluster, with a staged Helm-adoption playbook**" — a materially stronger line, and a natural companion writeup to the NemoClaw build (which watches this same Fleet delivery pipeline). 207

cd /Users/rashford/Developer/lab-fleet
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
Add Fleet bundles: wire lab-fleet for Rancher Continuous Delivery

Phase 1 of the fleet-wiring plan — make the repo GitOps-deployable by Rancher
Fleet. Adds a fleet.yaml to each deployable numbered dir:

- Helm bundles (takeOwnership, versions pinned to the running releases so Fleet
  adopts rather than upgrades): metallb 0.16.1, longhorn 1.12.0, nvdp 0.19.2,
  dcgm-exporter 4.8.2, ollama 1.67.0, open-webui 14.8.0, milvus 5.0.22.
- Raw-manifest bundles: comfyui, indexer, mcp, hermes, emby, node-red, resilio,
  plus metallb pools, the nvidia RuntimeClass, and attu.

Where a dir held a Helm chart plus raw manifests, the raw manifests move into a
sibling subdir so each chart owns its own bundle path (Fleet is one chart per
path): 01-networking/pools, 03-gpu/{dcgm-exporter,runtimeclass}, 06-milvus/attu.
README install paths updated to match.

The github-mcp token example is renamed .yaml -> .yaml.txt so Fleet never
applies the placeholder Secret. 00-host and 14-cluster-mgmt stay docs-only (no
fleet.yaml); Traefik is k3s-bundled and not Fleet-managed.

Phases 0/2/3 (enable Fleet CD on the Rancher controller, create the GitRepo,
staged rollout) require a Rancher API token and remain owner-run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "== committed =="; git log --oneline -1; echo; git show --stat --oneline HEAD | head -40
